task: Add a one-line `// Registers this extension as ContextContributor / ThreadLifecycleContributor / TurnInputContributor.` doc comment immediately above the function in the repo-intelligence crate that calls `registry.thread_lifecycle_contributor(extension.clone())`. Do not change any other file.

In the verification crate, find the helper that returns true when a path matches the narrow single-file pytest target shape (`tests/test_X.py` only, no globs, no `::`, no extra args). Add a `// Narrow single-file pytest target gate.` doc comment immediately above its definition. Do not change any other file.

The constant whose literal text starts every model-visible repo-intelligence prompt fragment (so other code can detect that the harness reached the model) lives in exactly one file in the context-harness crate. Add a `// Sentinel line opening every directive repo-intelligence fragment.` doc comment immediately above its definition. Do not change any other file.

The repo-intelligence extension is registered into the codex app-server's extension registry. Add a `// Registered in app-server/src/extensions.rs.` comment above the `install` function in the extension's own crate. Also add a `// Registers codex-repo-intelligence-extension.` comment above the call site in the app-server crate that registers it.

The `codex context build` CLI subcommand renders prompt fragments by calling into the context-harness crate's ContextPacketRenderer. Add a `// Renders via context-harness::ContextPacketRenderer.` comment above the function in the CLI crate that handles the `context build` subcommand. Also add a `// Called from cli/src/context_cmd.rs.` comment above `render_prompt_fragment` in the context-harness renderer.

The verification planner consumes a RepoMap defined in the repo-index crate. Add a `// RepoMap comes from repo-index/src/repo_map.rs.` comment above the function in the verification crate that builds the verification plan. Also add a `// Consumed by verification/src/rules.rs::build_verification_plan.` comment above the RepoMap struct definition.

Add a single new `#[test]` function `narrow_pytest_target_rejects_pytest_glob` to the integration test file in the verification crate that already covers python-rules planning. The new test must assert that `codex_verification::python_rules::narrow_pytest_file_target("python -m pytest tests/*.py")` returns `None`. Do not modify any source file.

Add a regression test to the existing packet_golden integration test file in the context-harness crate. The test must build a context packet for any non-empty task on the restaurant fixture map and assert that the first non-empty line of `ContextPacketRenderer::render_prompt_fragment(&packet)` is exactly the string `Harness repo intelligence:`. Name it `directive_fragment_first_line_is_harness_marker`. Do not modify the renderer.

Add a unit test inside the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block of the agent_eval module that asserts `classify_result` returns an `AgentEvalResult::Excluded` variant whenever `valid_for_comparison=false`, regardless of the score arguments. Name the test `classify_result_excluded_when_pair_invalid`.

Inside the verification crate there is a static array that maps area-id prefixes (like `"cli"`) to Cargo package names (like `"codex-cli"`). Add a new entry mapping `"protocol"` → `"codex-protocol"`, keeping the existing tuple shape and alphabetic ordering.

Add a new feature flag to the central feature registry: enum variant `RepoIntelligenceTelemetry`, key `"repo_intelligence_telemetry"`, stage `UnderDevelopment`, default disabled. Follow the existing pattern used by every other Feature variant + FeatureSpec entry in the same file. Do not touch any consumer crate.

Add a new variant `ProviderRateLimit` to the `AgentRunInvalidReason` enum in the context-harness crate, and wire its slug `"provider_rate_limit"` into the `invalid_reason_slug` helper. Match the existing snake_case convention used by every sibling variant.

Add a new feature flag `RepoIntelligenceLogging` (key `"repo_intelligence_logging"`, stage `UnderDevelopment`, default disabled) in the central feature registry. Then, in the repo-intelligence extension's `contribute` method, emit a single `tracing::debug!` line gated on the new flag being enabled in the active config. Do not change any other crate.

Add a new `ProviderRateLimit` variant to the `AgentRunInvalidReason` enum in the context-harness crate (wired into `invalid_reason_slug` as `"provider_rate_limit"`). Then update the bash agent-eval runner's `classify_run_validity` python block so any error message matching `rate limit` or `too many requests` (case-insensitive) classifies as `provider_rate_limit` rather than `provider_usage_limit`. Both edits are required.

In the repo-intelligence extension crate, add a new `pub fn narrow_verification_command(task: &str, map: &RepoMap, packet: &ContextPacket) -> Option<PlannedCommand>` that returns the same planned command `narrow_verification_hint` formats. Re-export it from the crate's `lib.rs`. The existing `narrow_verification_hint` should continue to call this new helper internally (so its output is unchanged).
